data.goal: audit_vibe_coding: start from a software idea such as build a HASHI Watchtower, record and drive the work all the way to a finished and tested product, including end-to-end flow and internal loops such as testing -> bug -> debug -> fix -> testing, and exit only when the whole idea is built and tested to be fully functional.

data.intent_summary: audit_vibe_coding: start from a software idea such as build a HASHI Watchtower, record and drive the work all the way to a finished and tested product, including end-to-end flow and internal loops such as testing -> bug -> debug -> fix -> testing, and exit only when the whole idea is built and tested to be fully functional.

audit_vibe_coding is a recording-first product delivery superloop. It begins with an idea, captures product boundaries and non-goals, turns the idea into an implementation plan, tracks nested build/test/debug/fix loops, requires end-to-end validation evidence, and exits only when the product is implemented, documented, and proven fully functional in real use.

data.title: Capture original idea, intended users, product boundaries, and explicit non-goals
Extract requirements and architecture constraints from Watchtower discussion records
Design minimal core with modular sidecar boundaries, narrow rescue API, and structured audit logging
Model explicit Windows, WSL, and remote target handling without guessing paths or runtime context
Break implementation into product phases, taskboard items, waits, issues, artifacts, and evidence checkpoints
Implement the smallest vertical slice that proves the end-to-end Watchtower flow
Run internal test loop: test -> bug found -> debug -> fix -> retest until green
Run real end-to-end smoke test across the intended target environment
Collect acceptance evidence: logs, test results, operator docs, and known limitations
Exit only after the whole idea is built, tested fully functional, and documented for operation

criteria: idea captured with product boundaries and non-goals
architecture keeps core minimal and features modular
Watchtower/rescue sidecar API is narrow, explicit, and auditable
Windows and WSL target handling is explicit and tested
internal testing-bug-debug-fix-retesting loops are recorded in events/issues/tasks
real end-to-end smoke test proves the product works in the intended environment
operator documentation and acceptance evidence exist
no known blocker remains for normal operation
